chunk_summary: Guidelines for empiric antibiotic regimens in severe acute malnutrition (SAM) emphasize reassessment after 48–72 hours if no clinical improvement is observed, focusing on missed infections, resistant organisms, or alternate diagnoses.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of empiric antibiotics in managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Learn the importance of reassessment after 48–72 hours of antibiotic therapy in SAM cases.
Identify potential causes of treatment failure, including missed infections or resistant organisms.
Familiarize with WHO and ICMR guidelines on facility-based management of SAM.
key_facts: Empiric antibiotics are recommended for children with complicated SAM to address potential infections.
Reassessment is critical after 48–72 hours if no clinical improvement is observed.
Missed infection focus, resistant organisms, or alternate diagnoses should be considered during reassessment.
WHO guidelines emphasize prevention and management of wasting and nutritional oedema in SAM.
ICMR STW provides specific protocols for severe acute malnutrition management in India.
reference_quote: If there is no clinical improvement after 48–72 hours, actively reassess for missed infection focus, resistant organisms, or an alternate diagnosis rather than simply 'continuing the same course.'
document_key_topics: WHO and ICMR guidelines on SAM management
